nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: 阿拉伯联合酋长国
nodes.summary: 阿拉伯联合酋长国是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Sand partridge、石鸡、大红鹳等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 鸡形目
nodes.summary: 鸡形目（Galliformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联298种鸟类，如眼斑冢雉、Red-billed brushturkey、Black-billed brushturkey等298种鸟类。

nodes.name: 雉科
nodes.summary: 雉科（Phasianidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联179种鸟类，如Udzungwa forest partridge、銹紅林鷓鴣、冠鷓鴣等179种鸟类。

nodes.name: 中东
nodes.summary: 中东是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Sand partridge、大红鹳、Yellow-legged gull等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 也门
nodes.summary: 也门是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括Sand partridge、Arabian partridge、Philby's partridge等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 沙特阿拉伯
nodes.summary: 沙特阿拉伯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括Sand partridge、Arabian partridge、石鸡等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 苏丹
nodes.summary: 苏丹是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括南非鸭、Sand partridge、Scaly spurfowl等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Sand partridge
nodes.summary: Sand partridge（Sand partridge，学名 Ammoperdix heyi），分类上属于鸡形目，并归入雉科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括滩涂，主要威胁包括气候变化、猎捕压力。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Sand partridges are endemic across the Middle East. Populations are distributed across Eastern Egypt, Eastern Sudan, Israel, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman, and the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The four subspecies of the sand partridge are native to specific parts of the Middle East

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Sand partridges can be found in desert ecosystems with scattered vegetation and boulders. They prefer steep, boulder-strewn slopes with sandy-bottomed wadis and ridges up to 2000 m and valleys down to 400 m below sea level. Sand partridges are rarely found in large stretches of dry, flat, or open desert as they require a water source for drinking. The sand partridge is non-migratory, so it breeds and spends its year in these habitats

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The sand partridge is listed as 'Least Concern' by the IUCN. The sand partridge has a large range and is deemed common and stable in the regions it resides. The sand partridge has been introduced to two islands offshore of Saudi Arabia and the UAE. The estimated entire population of sand partridge pairs in Arabia is ~900,000 and is increasing. Sand partridges live in areas that are mainly inhabitable to humans, thus have minimal threats. Throughout Arabia, stock grazing, urbanization, drought, and climate change in general, all affect sand partridges but are not true threats. Additionally, sand partridges are hunted for sport and food, locally and nationally. Over the entire range of the sand partridges habitats, there are conservation sites present. A non-profit in Jordan known as the Jordan Outdoor Sport Association was established in 2014 to encourage sustainable hunting in the area. Releasing sand partridges to supplement hunted birds is part of their conservation work
